story_text.value:
## Mac Users Got Left Out, Until Now

For individuals utilizing Microsoft Excel on Mac systems, a significant limitation has persisted: the absence of Microsoft’s Fuzzy Lookup Add-In, a feature restricted to Windows versions of Excel. This exclusion has hindered Mac users from leveraging fuzzy matching, a critical technique for identifying approximate matches within datasets, directly within their preferred spreadsheet software. This gap in functionality has compelled many to resort to inefficient workarounds or alternative platforms, often at the expense of productivity with budgetary considerations.

Flookup Data Wrangler emerges as an indispensable solution, specifically tailored to address this need for Mac users. Operating as an add-on for Google Sheets, Flookup delivers robust fuzzy matching capabilities alongside advanced data cleaning tools, making it an essential asset for professionals managing data on Mac devices. This article delineates why Flookup is a requisite tool for enhancing data workflows.

### The Significance of Fuzzy Matching in Data Management

Fuzzy matching is a sophisticated method designed to identify strings that are approximately similar rather than precisely identical. This technique proves invaluable in data management tasks such as deduplication, record linkage and the correction of inconsistent or misspelled entries. For instance, fuzzy matching can ascertain that “New York Inc.” and “NY Incorporated” likely represent the same entity despite textual disparities. Such functionality is crucial for professionals handling extensive datasets, including customer databases, financial records or research data where inaccuracies and variations are commonplace.

While Microsoft’s Fuzzy Lookup Add-In has provided Windows users with an integrated solution for fuzzy matching within Excel, its unavailability on Mac has left a notable void. Mac users, constrained by this limitation, have often been compelled to employ virtual machines or other suboptimal methods to access comparable tools, thereby complicating their workflows.

#### Core Features of Flookup

- Advanced Fuzzy Matching Capabilities: Flookup provides specialized functions, such as FUZZYMATCH and FLOOKUP, which compute similarity scores between text strings and execute lookups based on percentage similarity. This enables precise identification of approximate matches, accommodating errors such as typos, abbreviations or inconsistencies within datasets.

- Data Cleaning with AI: Beyond fuzzy matching, Flookup incorporates artificial intelligence to streamline data cleaning processes. It facilitates the detection of duplicates, standardization of data formats and management of complex cleaning tasks, reducing the reliance on manual efforts.

- Integration with Google Sheets: Operating within Google Sheets, Flookup capitalizes on the platform’s collaborative features and cloud accessibility. This integration is particularly advantageous for teams operating remotely or across diverse devices.

### Why Flookup Is Good for Mac Users

For Mac users previously excluded from the advantages of fuzzy matching within Excel, Flookup Data Wrangler represents an essential tool that not only resolves this deficiency but also elevates data management capabilities. Its compatibility with Google Sheets ensures operational efficiency across devices, while its advanced features deliver a level of precision and sophistication surpassing basic fuzzy matching.

In an era where data accuracy and operational efficiency are paramount, Flookup is a necessity for Mac users managing critical datasets like customer records, financial ledgers or research compilations. This tool empowers users to organize and refine their data with unparalleled ease and accuracy.

WEBSITE: https://www.getflookup.com

INSTALL: https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/flookup/593806014962

title.value: Fuzzy Matching on Mac: Missing No More?

High-volume applications with low-quality filtering. Fragmented hiring workflows across spreadsheets and emails. Poor collaboration between recruiters, hiring managers, and interviewers. Lack of data insights, making recruitment decisions reactive rather than strategic. The Solution: AI-Driven Applicant Tracking An Applicant Tracking System (ATS) solves these inefficiencies by automating candidate sourcing, resume screening, interview scheduling, and recruitment analytics.

MokaHR is a modern, AI-powered ATS that helps companies scale hiring through: Automated resume parsing & intelligent ranking to filter the best candidates. Customizable hiring workflows that adapt to different recruitment needs. Data-driven insights on hiring efficiency, time-to-fill, and candidate drop-off. WeChat & mobile integration for a seamless hiring experience in China.

Why MokaHR? Unlike legacy ATS platforms like Workday or Taleo, MokaHR is:

Designed for speed → Minimal learning curve, quick implementation. AI-enhanced → Smart recommendations reduce manual screening. Optimized for China → Local job board integration, compliance support, WeChat hiring. Built for collaboration → Real-time feedback loops between HR, hiring managers, and recruiters. Who is Using It? MokaHR is trusted by fast-scaling startups, mid-sized companies, and enterprises that need a modern hiring system. Whether you’re hiring 10 or 10,000 employees, optimizing the recruitment funnel is critical.

Hiring is a competitive advantage. If you’re still relying on outdated tools, you’re already behind.

Learn more: MokaHR.io

story_text.value:
Hi hn, over the years I have interacted with the stock market as well as cryptocurrencies but I have never put any serious effort on tracking this portfolio.

While I have saw that there are many portfolio tracker apps, I haven't been able to find anything that meets my requirements, then, I ended up with spreadsheets (which are helpful but they have its own drawbacks).

Have you seen anything covering most of these scenarios?

1. Support the traditional stock market as well as cryptocurrencies.

2. There is no need to link any stock services or crypto wallets, data can be entered manually or imported from CSV files.

3. Support for multiple currencies (USD, EUR, etc).

4. Support for including fees as well as dividends.

5. Data can be exported.

6. Data is stored in a server but it is encrypted at the client, this means that the server can't read it.

Thanks.

title.value: Ask HN: Is there any encrypted portfolio tracker available?

story_text.value:
You didn't build a startup to do bookkeeping... did you?

Are you building a startup? That’s my jam—I LOVE startups. I built (and sold) my first one not too long ago, and let me tell you: the part I absolutely hated was the back-office busywork.

You know the drill: bookkeeping, spreadsheets, receipts...Ugh. I didn’t start a company to watch QuickBooks load—I started it because I had a mission and a passion.

So here’s the twist: this time around, I built a whole new startup dedicated to bookkeeping. Why? Because founders like you and me shouldn’t waste another second on all that administrative nonsense. Focus on building product, closing deals, and making waves—we’ll handle the rest.

Seriously. Let’s get you back to doing what you love (and crush it). Leave the spreadsheets to us.

email me ! info@booklio.co

story_text.value:
Are there any datasets, with any labels or no labels, for people using their desktop computers to do mundane tasks like open applications, browse the web, interact with spreadsheets, etc?

I was thinking this type of desktop "screen record" data could be useful for training AI Agents in the future.

It seems the hardest part of getting AI to "just do work" on my laptop is being able to navigate different applications, copy and paste stuff, trouble shoot weird one-off interactions like software updates moving some buttons/features to different places, etc. Rather than trying to programmatically figure everything out from e.g. an excel API to do spreadsheet work.

title.value: Ask HN: Datasets for computer desktop usage, AI Agents discussion
